title: Dispute resolution myths: True or false?
description: This article is the second in a three-part series on judicial transformation based on the experience of PUCAR (Public Collective for Avoidance and Resolution of Disputes).Every reform runs on beliefs it has never examined. In judicial reform, these beliefs are particularly consequential, because they determine who the process is designed for, who is assumed to be the obstacle and what kind of change is even considered possible.These beliefs are rarely stated. They don't need to be, because they
